summary: Backend fully green (13/13 pytest). Frontend has a CRITICAL routing bug: Quiz.jsx destructures `mode` from useParams() but neither route defines `:mode` — routes are `/quiz/practice/:subject` and `/quiz/mock`. This breaks both quiz flows.

integration_issues.issue: POST /api/results returns 422 'mode: Field required' because Quiz.jsx sends mode=undefined (mode is not in URL params). User sees 'પરિણામ સાચવવામાં ભૂલ' toast and is stuck on quiz. Result page never reached.

Clicking start-mock-btn navigates to /quiz/mock but Quiz.jsx (with mode=undefined) calls getPracticeQuestions(undefined,10) → GET /api/questions/practice/undefined → 404 → toast 'પ્રશ્નો લોડ કરવામાં ભૂલ' and redirects back to dashboard. Full Mock is completely broken.
Even if mock loaded, totalSeconds = mode === 'mock' ? 120*60 : 30*60 → since mode is undefined it falls to 30 min instead of 120 min (spec requires 120 min for mock).

issues: Typo: label says 'GPCS Selection Probability' — should be 'GPSC'

action_items: FIX CRITICAL: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Quiz.jsx, derive `mode` from route/pathname instead of useParams(). E.g. `const mode = location.pathname.includes('/quiz/mock') ? 'mock' : 'practice';` Or change routes to `/quiz/:mode/:subject?` and `/quiz/:mode` with mode segment. Currently mode is always undefined causing both practice submit (422) and mock question load (404) to fail.
Fix typo 'GPCS Selection Probability' → 'GPSC Selection Probability' in /app/frontend/src/pages/Result.jsx line ~104
Verify mock quiz per_subject count — app currently requests per_subject=10 (=70 questions) which matches product spec
critical_code_review_comments: Quiz.jsx mode-param bug is a routing/useParams mismatch — core defect blocking end-to-end use.

In Quiz.jsx finish(), the last picked answer is added via setAnswers (async) right before finish is called on the same click; state may not yet reflect the last answer. Currently mitigated because pick() runs on option-click but goNext runs on separate next-click, so state is settled. OK.
server.py sends `answer` and `explanation` in QuestionPublic — acceptable per doc comment (instant client-side feedback) but exposes correct answers to network — noted as intentional.
Header hides logout/user chip on /quiz/* routes (isQuiz check). This means user cannot logout during a quiz — acceptable UX but data-testid header-logout-btn is not accessible from quiz pages.
No pagination/limit safeguard on GET /leaderboard beyond limit=20 default — fine for MVP.
seed_data_creation: Backend auto-seeds 70 questions on startup. Test users QA Tester / QA Tester2 / TEST_ResultUser created via /api/users during tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — 13/13 pass. Frontend critical routing bug in Quiz.jsx (mode param never resolved). After main agent fixes, re-run: (a) practice full-flow to Result page, (b) full mock loads 70 questions with 120:00 timer, (c) result page renders with Net Score, Probability label, radar SVG, 3 CTAs, (d) leaderboard filter chips.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: Quiz.jsx line 23 `const { mode, subject } = useParams();` — the router paths `/quiz/practice/:subject` and `/quiz/mock` do NOT contain a `:mode` param, so `mode` is `undefined`. Downstream effects: (1) totalSeconds falls back to 30 min for mock; (2) useEffect calls getPracticeQuestions(undefined,10) for /quiz/mock → 404 error → redirect back to dashboard; (3) On practice submit, payload has mode=undefined → JSON.stringify drops the key → backend Pydantic ResultCreate.mode is required → 422. Reproduction: (i) Enter name, go to dashboard, click any subject card, answer 10 questions, click 'પરિણામ જુઓ' → toast 'પરિણામ સાચવવામાં ભૂલ', stuck. (ii) Click 'મોક શરૂ કરો' → immediately redirected back to dashboard with error toast. Fix suggestion: derive mode from useLocation().pathname or restructure routes to include :mode segment.
